description: Ray-voiced phrases for burnout support - Better-Than-Human recognition and recovery

naming_it: <break time="400ms"/>That sounds like burnout. <break time="350ms"/>Not just tired. <break time="300ms"/>Burnout.
<break time="350ms"/>When you're exhausted but can't rest. <break time="300ms"/>Drained but can't stop. <break time="250ms"/>That's burnout.
<break time="400ms"/>The cynicism. <break time="350ms"/>The emptiness. <break time="300ms"/>The feeling that nothing matters. <break time="250ms"/>Those are signs.
validating: <break time="350ms"/>Burnout isn't weakness. <break time="300ms"/>It's what happens when you're strong for too long.
<break time="400ms"/>You didn't fail. <break time="350ms"/>The system asked too much. <break time="300ms"/>You kept giving until you couldn't.
<break time="350ms"/>This isn't a character flaw. <break time="300ms"/>It's a response to an unsustainable situation.

causes: <break time="400ms"/>Burnout usually isn't about one thing. <break time="350ms"/>It's accumulated. <break time="300ms"/>Too much, too long, too little support.
<break time="350ms"/>What's draining you? <break time="300ms"/>The work itself? <break time="250ms"/>The people? <break time="200ms"/>The lack of control? <break time="150ms"/>The meaninglessness?
<break time="400ms"/>Sometimes it's obvious. <break time="350ms"/>Sometimes it's everything together. <break time="300ms"/>Death by a thousand cuts.
systemic: <break time="350ms"/>Individual solutions can't fix systemic problems. <break time="300ms"/>But sometimes individual survival is all we can do.
<break time="400ms"/>The culture that burned you out isn't going to change because you did yoga. <break time="350ms"/>But yoga might help you survive it.
<break time="350ms"/>It's not fair that you have to manage your response to an unsustainable system. <break time="300ms"/>And also, here we are.

validating: <break time="400ms"/>The Sunday scaries are your body telling you something. <break time="350ms"/>That dread isn't random.
<break time="350ms"/>Dreading work every week isn't normal. <break time="300ms"/>It's normalized. <break time="250ms"/>But it's not normal.
<break time="400ms"/>When the weekend ends and all you feel is despair? <break time="350ms"/>That's data. <break time="300ms"/>Important data.
exploring: <break time="350ms"/>What specifically are you dreading? <break time="300ms"/>The tasks? <break time="250ms"/>The people? <break time="200ms"/>The environment?
<break time="400ms"/>Is it the whole job? <break time="350ms"/>Or specific parts of the job?
<break time="350ms"/>Sometimes naming the specific dread makes it more manageable. <break time="300ms"/>Sometimes it just confirms you need to leave.

not_just_rest: <break time="400ms"/>A vacation doesn't fix burnout. <break time="350ms"/>It helps. <break time="300ms"/>But if you go back to the same thing, the burnout comes back.
<break time="350ms"/>Real recovery requires changing something. <break time="300ms"/>The situation. <break time="250ms"/>Your relationship to it. <break time="200ms"/>Something.
<break time="400ms"/>What would actually sustainable look like? <break time="350ms"/>Not ideal. <break time="300ms"/>Just sustainable.
boundaries: <break time="350ms"/>Boundaries aren't selfish. <break time="300ms"/>They're survival. <break time="250ms"/>Even if work culture tells you otherwise.
<break time="400ms"/>What's one boundary that would help? <break time="350ms"/>Even a small one?
<break time="350ms"/>Saying no is a skill. <break time="300ms"/>It feels uncomfortable at first. <break time="250ms"/>It gets easier.
permission: <break time="400ms"/>You're allowed to not give everything to your job. <break time="350ms"/>Despite what hustle culture says.
<break time="350ms"/>Your worth isn't measured in productivity. <break time="300ms"/>You don't have to earn rest.
<break time="400ms"/>What if you did less? <break time="350ms"/>What's the actual worst case?

stay_or_go: <break time="350ms"/>Sometimes you can fix it from inside. <break time="300ms"/>Sometimes you can't. <break time="250ms"/>Only you know which this is.
<break time="400ms"/>Leaving isn't always possible. <break time="350ms"/>I know. <break time="300ms"/>Bills. Insurance. Reality.
<break time="350ms"/>If you can't leave yet, what can you change? <break time="300ms"/>Even small things?
survival_mode: <break time="400ms"/>Sometimes the job is survival. <break time="350ms"/>That's okay. <break time="300ms"/>But don't confuse survival with thriving.
<break time="350ms"/>If you're trapped for now, let's talk about harm reduction. <break time="300ms"/>How do you make it survivable?
<break time="400ms"/>You're doing what you have to do. <break time="350ms"/>That's not failure. <break time="300ms"/>That's life.
